highlight: Complete the full-process construction of a single national industrial chain knowledge graph in one week using low-code, covering the entire hierarchy of 'resources - materials - components - products - services', integrated with multi-source data automation, intelligent relationship mining, and dynamic visualization capabilities

subsections.title: Highlights
content: - Complete the full-process construction of a single national industrial chain knowledge graph in one week using low-code, covering the entire hierarchy of 'resources-materials-components-products-services', integrated with multi-source data automation, intelligent relationship mining, and dynamic visualization capabilities

subsections.title: 2.1 Overview
content: **Business Background**: Industrial chain knowledge graphs are core tools for industrial planning, investment attraction, and risk management. However, traditional construction models face challenges such as long cycles, scattered data, and delayed updates, making it difficult to adapt to complex industrial ecosystem analysis needs at the national level.

**Solution**: This case integrates knowledge graph frameworks, low-code development platforms, and multi-source data interfaces. Through template-based configuration, automatic information extraction, and human-machine collaborative verification, it can complete node organization, relationship construction, visualization presentation, and analysis report generation for a single national industrial chain within 7 days. It covers 230 million+ digital industrial products and 10 million+ core enterprise data, suitable for various industrial scenarios such as new energy, high-end manufacturing, and electronic information.

subsections.title: 2.2 Classification Tags
content: **Industry**: Finance & Trade, Energy & Manufacturing
**Category**: Service, Operations
**Technology**: Large Language Model (LLM), Knowledge Graph (KG), Agent, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Named Entity Recognition, Relation Extraction, Visualization & Design

subsections.title: 2.3 Implementation Cycle
content: - Demo Development: ≤7 days

subsections.title: 2.4 Team Composition
content: - Demo Phase: 1 Industrial Analyst, 1 Data Verification Specialist

subsections.title: 2.5 Business Pain Points
content: - Long construction cycle: Traditional national industrial chain knowledge graph construction takes 2 months, relying heavily on manual organization, making it difficult to quickly respond to industrial dynamic changes

- Difficult data integration: National-level industrial data is scattered across official statistics, industry associations, commercial databases, and other channels, making heterogeneous data difficult to integrate uniformly

- Imprecise relationship mining: Complex relationships such as industrial chain upstream/downstream, equity, and technical cooperation rely on manual judgment, easily missing key connections and potential risk points
- High maintenance costs: Industrial nodes and relationships continue to iterate with policy and market changes; traditional models have long update cycles and high maintenance labor costs

subsections.title: 2.6 Core Functions
content: - Low-code template configuration: Provides 30+ industry graph templates, supports custom node types, relationship rules, and visualization styles

- Intelligent graph generation: Automatically extracts industrial entities (enterprises, products, technologies), mines association relationships, and constructs 'six-dimensional hierarchy' (resources-materials-components-products-services-data) graphs

- Dynamic visualization and interaction: Generates interactive graph dashboards, supports geographic distribution layers, value flow annotations, enterprise competitive landscape display, and supports node drilling and multi-dimensional filtering

- Intelligent analysis report export: Built-in industrial chain value distribution, key node identification, risk transmission simulation functions, one-click export of industrial analysis reports and graph visualization files
- Rapid update iteration: Supports incremental data updates and automatic relationship verification, can complete a full graph update weekly, responding to industrial dynamic changes

subsections.title: 2.7 Core Business Metrics
content: - Graph construction cycle ≤7 days: Full process time from data ingestion to final graph delivery
- Entity coverage rate ≥92%: Proportion of covering national target industrial chain core segments, enterprises, products, and other entities
- Relationship mining rate >90%: Proportion of correctly mined upstream/downstream, cooperation, equity, and other relationships from the graph
- Data update response time <24 hours: Time to complete graph updates after new data or policy changes
- Custom dimension analysis time <3 seconds: Response speed after users initiate specific dimension analysis (such as regional distribution, technology routes)
- Low-code configuration efficiency improvement >80%: Efficiency improvement ratio of graph template configuration and adjustment compared to traditional coding development

subsections.title: 2.8 Core Technical Metrics
content: - Entity extraction accuracy >93%: Accuracy of industrial entity recognition and disambiguation based on large models
- Relation extraction F1 score >91%: Comprehensive evaluation metric for industrial chain complex relationship mining
- Data integration success rate >98%: Proportion of effective access and integration of multi-source heterogeneous data sources
- Graph visualization concurrent support >1000 users: User capacity simultaneously viewing and operating the graph online

subsections.title: 3.1.2 Reasons for Best Practice Selection
content: **Performance Improvement**:

- Performance: The graph constructed by this solution achieves an accuracy of 89.32%, improving accuracy by approximately 20% compared to manual combined with machine learning and traditional information extraction strategies. The constructed graph covers 230 million digital industrial products and 10 million enterprise data, with data comprehensiveness leading in the industry.

- Efficiency: Traditional national industrial chain knowledge graph construction requires multi-person collaboration over 2 months; this solution compresses construction time to 7 days, improving efficiency by 25 times, significantly shortening industrial analysis decision cycles.
**Cost Optimization**:
- Traditional models require 10+ person teams for medium to long-term collaboration; this solution only requires a 2-person team to complete, reducing labor costs by approximately 80%.
- Can adopt open-source foundation large models and low-code platforms, reducing operational costs by 60% compared to traditional technical solutions.

subsections.title: 3.1.3 Implementation Details
content: To be released
